items.explain: は marks the topic of the sentence.
を marks the direct object of a transitive verb.
に marks the destination with movement verbs (いく/くる/かえる).
で marks the place where an action happens.
と marks the person you do something together with.
は marks the topic 'this'.
が marks the subject of existence (います/あります).
に marks a specific point in time.
で marks the means or method (transport, tools).
は sets 'my book' as the topic being asked about.
With 〜たい, the object often takes が (を is also acceptable).
に marks the destination for かえる.

items.sentence: にほんご＿じょうずです。
items.explain: じょうず/へた/すき take が for the thing you are good at / like.

items.after: ばんまではたらきます。
items.en: I work from morning until night.
items.explain: から marks the starting point (time or place).

items.en: I walk up to the station.
items.explain: まで marks the end point of movement or time.

items.en: I take a walk through the park.
items.explain: を marks the path traversed with motion verbs (さんぽする/あるく/わたる).
も means 'also/too', replacing は/が.
へ marks direction of movement (interchangeable with に here).

items.after: プレゼントをもらいました。
items.en: I received a present from my father.
items.explain: に marks the giver/source with もらう.

items.sentence: この りょうり＿つくりました。
items.explain: を marks the direct object of an action.
が marks the subject of a natural phenomenon.

items.sentence: コーヒー＿おちゃをのみます。
items.explain: か between nouns means 'or'.

items.en: I ask the teacher a question.
items.explain: に marks the person the action is directed to.

items.sentence: にく＿やさいをかいました。
items.explain: と links nouns as a complete 'and' list.
